open Classical in
/-- The original `exists_vcn_le_of_convex_rn_add_one` is false at `n = 0`: a convex set in ℝ¹
can trivially 0-shatter the singleton type `Fin 0 → ℕ`. -/
lemma exists_vcn_le_of_convex_rn_add_one_counterexample :
    ¬ (∀ (C : Set (Fin 1 → ℝ)),
      Convex ℝ C →
        ∀ (x : Fin 0 → ℕ → Fin 1 → ℝ) (y : Set (Fin 0 → ℕ) → Fin 1 → ℝ),
          (∀ (i : Fin 0 → ℕ) (s : Set (Fin 0 → ℕ)), ∑ k, x k (i k) + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s) → False) := by
  push_neg
  set e : Fin 0 → ℕ := Fin.elim0
  refine ⟨{v | v 0 > 0}, ?_, Fin.elim0, fun s => fun _ => if e ∈ s then 1 else -1, ?_, trivial⟩
  · exact convex_iff_forall_pos.mpr (fun x hx y hy a b ha hb hab => by
      simp [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at *
      positivity)
  · intro i s
    simp only [Finset.univ_eq_empty, Finset.sum_empty]
    have : i = e := by ext x; exact Fin.elim0 x
    subst this
    simp only [zero_add, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    split_ifs with h
    · constructor
      · intro; exact h
      · intro; norm_num
    · constructor
      · intro h2; norm_num at h2
      · intro h2; exact absurd h2 h

/-- Corrected conjecture: Every convex set in ℝⁿ⁺¹ has finite VCₙ dimension, for n ≥ 1.

The modification from the original is the addition of the hypothesis `0 < n`, which
excludes the trivially false n = 0 case. -/
lemma exists_vcn_le_of_convex_rn_add_one (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
    ∃ d : ℕ, ∀ (C : Set (Fin (n + 1) → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : Fin n → ℕ → Fin (n + 1) → ℝ)
      (y : Set (Fin n → ℕ) → Fin (n + 1) → ℝ) (hxy : ∀ i s, ∑ k, x k (i k) + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s),
        False := sorry
